title: DetectionPoint
description: Canonical detection point (PRD §5.4). Every API response that carries detection points, every bundle manifest entry, and every on-device store row uses exactly these ten fields, verbatim. This schema is the single source of truth; the OpenAPI DetectionPoint component and the Kotlin DetectionPoint data class mirror it.

point_code.description: Stable machine identifier, unique within a standard revision. Accepts the code styles already in use: UPPER_SNAKE (e.g. PEARL_COUNT), hyphenated (e.g. DP-FLOWER-FRONT-001, REQ-STAIN-001), and their lowercase variants (e.g. dp-bracelet-001).

label.description: Short human-readable name shown to operators (localizable display text).

description.description: Fuller explanation of what this checkpoint inspects. May be null/absent for terse points.

method_hint.description: How to inspect this point (e.g. 'count pearls in the center cluster'). May be null/absent.

expected_value.description: Expected observed value where meaningful, e.g. '3' for a count checkpoint. Null when not applicable.

pass_criteria.description: Explicit criterion that must hold for a pass. May be null/absent when the pass condition is implicit (e.g. 'no defect visible').

evidence_required.description: If true, a captured evidence image is required to record a result for this point.

regions.description: Optional spatial grounding (PRD Authoring Extension §2). Zero, one, or many bounding boxes marking where this detection point applies on the SKU's standard photos. Absent or empty is valid — a point may rely on its natural-language description and thresholds alone.

region.description: A single normalized bounding box on one standard photo. Coordinates are fractions of the image width/height with a top-left origin, so they are resolution-independent.

image_id.description: Id of the standard photo this box is drawn on.

severity.description: Defect severity if this point fails.

requiredView.description: Camera view the capture must present for this point. 'any' imposes no constraint.

incidentalFindingPolicy.description: How incidental findings near this checkpoint affect the verdict. flag_for_review escalates the item to review_required; record_only logs without changing the verdict; ignore drops them.
